column36: Comments
Right place, wrong time worked incredibly well with the excessive sidekicks produced by Magic. Tie that together with some strategic unleashing of the red guardian created massive damage.

Luckily had enough elite assassins  come out so Indestructible Man didn't hold me up. Hiriom did his job. Joe Fixit was supposed to facilitate she-hulk and bribe the high attack villains, he didn't show up much in the first game. Would've been faster but was one attack short for the MM several times and the extra 5 goblins didn't help either.

Jade Giantess revealed 12 cards for 15 atk, followed by Wield the Soulsword, so i finished JJJ, Indestructible and Madelyne (twice) in one turn.
Howard revealed 11 bystanders during the game and rescued 0, the traitor ^^
Hoped for Howard and Magik to do better with Man-Thing, but Namor an She-Hulk are very good together and won us the game.

column36: Heroes given where very strong, Howard + Magic giving him general sidekicks is already strong, and I added Red Guardian. She Hulk is also very good, and I maxed her use by adding Amadeus for even more Outwit. Setup itself is also great, just indestructible man gave problems, as assassins refused to come out.

Two very similar games, stat-wise, even down to the same points difference between players.  Things were flipped by the deck strategies, though.  The first attempt, Hiroim took top marks with support from Howard and Magik, but the second attempt She-Hulk backed by Amadeus an a smattering of others took the honors.  Tells me both were solid strategies, it was only down to HQ luck that made one come together better in a given game.
Built a covert deck, and a Recuit/smash deck. Game would have gone far faster if the Elite Assassins hadn't been so late to the party!!

This was challenging and fun. First game was disappointing. Scheme twists came out to quick and we lost just as our engine was getting started. Honestly felt a little shaky with our picks and so we went with bagel’s stream idea of namor and namora which went better. The game really could go sideways quick sir song on the timing of scheme twists and random mastermind placement. It was fun but very random. I feel like 2nd game could have gone south just as quick depending on how the villain deck shuffled out. She hulk was an all star and threw A LOT of trucks with her transformed card this go round.
Brought a bunch of extra 3-cost cards with Wolfsbane and Mirage to help make M even better. Worked pretty well!!!

column36: One hand went blue and the other focused mainly on M. Lots of XMen and blue for synergy. Ended up dead even between the hands on points.
Not a fan of schemes or MMs that remove cards from my deck, and this had both.
Professor X's rare is a great one for this Scheme; nabbing Villains as Heroes that can't be sent to the zoo feels like an inverse of the Scheme itself

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Early strikes kept the mastermind weak but he still got way stronger than I liked. Thanks to early clone I was able to recruit M's entire hero deck. I was playing a 3 cost spidey deck with karma and mirage supporting.

Game one had a quick overrun from sinister clones making the MM unreachable coupled with no rare cards showing up.
Game two did much better, M/Wolfsbane hit Belasco turn 6 and could never replicate the big hit on him well Jean/Karma was able to clean house coming just shy of a triple MM hit one turn. The city was much easier to control.
Skin of our teeth. We sat at 4 heroes in zoo for so long. Green is the way to go. Regret strong guy. He was too expensive.
Having the worst draws both game do not help with the fun level

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Schedule crunch + not sure how to compliment the heroes. Might have been able to do so with more time. Probably my worst week so far.

My wife always likes a set up that lets her get rid of starters - this time they were worth points too! She went heavily into Shield Level both times, once getting Mockingbird's ramping uncommon and once Coulson's. She did the majority of damage to Maria. I mostly kept the City clear.
Game 1 we lost on twist 7 which appeared on the turn my wife could have done the final blow. On game 2 we'd been able to delay til twist 8 but won before twist 7 anyway.
We had three unlucky escapes in our two games - one to a strike, one to an Ambush, one to a strike after her Fill The City tactic. Each time we'd cleared one spot but needed 2. Ah well, at least we won!
I love it when the Shield heroes get to work together. Just a shame that I couldn't also use Black Widow, Agent X13 or Rick Jones here for the full Shield team!

Fun setup. I finally got a chance to try Iceman... and he was terrible! First game I had a little luck with him, but Speedball's rare did more work than all of Iceman's cards. Second game I got super unlucky with twists, burying nearly all of Iceman's cards and leaving me with only 1 HQ space by turn 25. Mockingbird's uncommon completely carried that game.

Game 1 was 6 turns faster and a bit better on PPT, but with 2 escapes. Game 2 was slow with no escapes. I don't think either game was very good, but I went with game 2.

Maria is a really frustrating mastermind. The whole setup really depended on the arrangement of the villain deck, and also the hero deck. 
I picked Bishop and Wolverine who could provide extra KO and card draw. In first game they turned out to be really good picks. Unfortunately by turn 10 I already had 2 (sort of inevitable) escapes, as I got unlocky with starting hands, strong villains, and early master strikes. Then Bishop KO and an influx of henchmen in midgame helped creating efficient decks, resulting in a relatively quick win against Maria. 
Second game went totally different. I got lucky with early henchmen, but HQ had very bad heroes (Quake recruit cards), while getting lot of master strikes and shield elites. So by midgame my hands got overcrowded by Officers. While there were some stong turns, the final master strike still filled the city, resulting in an escape. Got quite high shield level, however. So both game had around 2.6 VP/turn, but first game had 26 turns, 2 escape. Second game had 34 turns, 1 escape. We'll see wheter I made the good choice by submitting the first one.  I guess 1 escape will be common, but also the 8 turn difference can mean a lot.

A nail-biter down to the last card in the villain deck.  I won quicker my first game, but with a lot of escaped villains and not nearly as many undercover points.  I really liked playing all the SHIELD heroes together.  Fun setup and very thematic.  I chose Ghost Rider and Punisher from Dark City because Ghost Rider was in a season of Agents of Shield, and Punisher just fit better thematically than the other MK heroes from Dark City.

So much SHIELD! This one escalated quickly and I had some early escapes, but things settled in nicely once SHIELD levels hit their targets. Headlok and Battle Staves delivered their extra cards, and Iceman and Quake had some nice range synergy. Iceman's rare was the story in the game as it allowed for enough attack to clear SHIELD villains from the city and allow for double SHIELD clearance for the Maria hit. Whole family's been super busy, so these games were all AntDude.

Tough match. Had a decent game 1 with Forge, despite losing BFG to the Scheme. Switched to Ant-Man for game 2, which I think was better, despite being diluted by the HQ, but I just got a tougher shuffle and couldn’t get any of the KO/Undercover combos going early enough, so that game ended slower with more escapes, and annoyingly with substantially more VP. Still, I’ll trade that for doing better on speed and escapes and submit match 1.

I enjoyed this game but I felt it got a little repetitive. The interaction between all the Shield characters was a lot of fun. Jocasta fit in really nice, I do wish I had seen some more of Forges cards but that is what happens. This was a hard decision for me to decide which game to take. One had less turns but more villains escape and less overall points earned but a higher points per turn average. While the other one had less villains escape and a few more turns.

A challenging setup that we had trouble with, mainly with the Villain escapes leading to spots in the HQ getting destroyed. We figured we could only have 1 escape to survive until the 8th twist in this setup, but Maria and her officers were intent on getting out of the city. Ugh she's a frustrating Mastermind. If we could've gotten to 1 more Twist I think we would've pulled out a Victory, but that was not in the cards for us this time.

Hitting Adrian Toomes as much as possible before getting Master Strikes, made both of my games fast.  Maximizing usage of Danger Sense helped keep the Master Strikes in the Villain Deck.  I chose the faster of my two games at the expense of a bit of ppt.  We'll see if it pays off or not.

For us the hero choice was a difficult one this week. No obvious standouts but a few which might have worked out. We ended up going with Fantastic Four United for the focus strategy and Brainstorm to pair with Princess Shuri. We were hoping to play a real fast and furious game to prevent having too many masterminds, but the hero deck was not in our favour. Princess Shuri hid in the deck for both our games. So did the rares for our first game - which was our best. We were surprised how much we ended up using Happy Hogan - especially for his Co-ordinate. This tournament is great for being given heroes which we haven't used much before or for a few years. We loved how you paired the villains to the mastermind to make it extra challenging. After our first game we were a bit more disappointed that we hadn't won quicker but then our second game took us 5 turns longer so we are hoping 26 turns wasn't as slow as it felt.

What a great excuse to break out more Homecoming - don't often play that set and it took me a full game to game the solo mechanics of coordinate.  Great time using Happy and Psi-Lord to go scheme hunting.  With the ascending masterminds, really added the jeopardy!

Ironically enough, I haven't played a lot of Homecoming as of late as I ain't too fussy about the MCU art on the cards. However, since Toomes is only 5 attack early on. I wanted to try and beat him up as much as possible with Michelle's powerful ability of fighting the Mastermind with recruit. This allowed me to fight him twice in one go early on before he got out of hand. 

From there, it was just trying to clean up the ascended Masterminds to get the win at a respectively 24 turns. I'm not sure if Brainstorm was the right pick for the other hand, as I went with a tech and range synergy that felt like it was constantly going out of time but not sure if it technically did anything but durdle around too much haha. I suspect 24 is good, but could be a middle of the pack in terms of turn orders unless those Master Strikes come out early!

I really liked this setup, the only thing that was a rough spot for me was facing the Giants again because of their type of disruption.  I tried to lean into the Danger Sense so that I wouldn't get overrun by Masterminds so Holland fit with Happy and Psi-Lord; Shuri and White Wolf paired up for Tech and Ambush triggers.  Turned out one side went almost full Instinct and Danger Sense'd to a clean city and smooth win, only hiccup was three quick Strikes near the beginning so I didn't get any cheap hits on Adrian.
Lots of strategy involved when deciding when to fight the mastermind or get victory points. Happy Hogan was good and bad depending on when those master strikes came out! Fun!

Usually if I play through a setup twice, I end up with two vastly different games. Not the case with this one. Both #1 and #2 were the almost the exact same. Same # of turns, within a few VP of each other, heck even the hands looked kind of identical. A bunch of early master strikes didn't too much to ding my hands too badly, but it made tougher to get the necessary attack to hit the mastermind, which added to my turn count for sure.
Good thing it wasn't Epic.

Had to play my games pretty quickly on day 1 because I am going for a vacation for a week. I was pretty satisfied with my hero choices, they played pretty great together. I was a little worried that I won't be able to make use of the shield levels with just Mockingbird, but I got the rare and a few of her other cards and that seemed to be useful enough. Bruce's and Hulkbuster's 2 costs made me draw a bunch of extra cards. Also the scheme seemed to help more than hurt me with the villains with Last stand. My strategy was to fill up the city with weaker villains not to give a chance for the Last stand effects to get ouf of hand, that way I was able to defeat them without any problem really. When the city and the VD felt comfortable enough, I was able to defeat the weaker villains and henchmen in the city to make it empty and finish the MM. Fun game, can't wait to see the results of my pod mates!

First game was fun, had an instinct deck that was really powerful by the end but the second game was one of the craziest I've ever played. So many turns with massive attack but not being able to hit the mastermind because of enemies in the city. Had to fight a 18 attack Headman Char because he turned up when I had two big victory piles. Also managed to have 4/5 rares on one team and it was right down to the final turn. A lot of fun but not as good as the first attempt haha. Skaar felt out of place, could have done more to work with him but the other heroes worked together surprisingly well

column36: Probably the most fun game I've played against the Red King. Smash + Hyperspeed is a fun combo. I decided to support Speed with my hero choices instead of going with support for Mockingbird and it paid off.

We tried to hard focus on Shield Level, but our card draw failed us in both games. Nothing worked out as we planned. Feasted our good cards away, bad luck drawing cards. As soon we got the legendary cards, it became nice and fast.

The combination of Dark Avengers with Last Stand and The Red King not being able to be attacked while Villans are in the city was a tough combo. Lots of KOing of cards was nice, by the last few turns we were rolling and barley scrapped out a win on literally last possible turn.

column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Last Stand villains in the HQ were tricky!  Fun stuff!  Mockingbird + Rick Jones was great.

Went with Korg since I figured his rare might be able to get some large attack values from the villains in the HQ. I managed to buy him but it only really got one decent turn though it did help in clearing the villains from the HQ.

This setup felt like slogging through mud. The HQ got bogged down the first attempt and then the second attempt saw a ton of wounds in the first 10 turns. Both games ended in a similar number of turns. I chose the first, where Mockingbird scaling carried the day against the Red King while a Skaar/Gladiator Hulk deck played Street Sweeper. The second had less defined deck development but more balance.

column36: It was weird and challenging but interesting, I definitely don't like wounded fury or Skaar very much but I like the combination of the scheme and last stand, felt very interesting.

column36: piplup1112111 played this setup
Don't really like the villain game and the Uru Weapons, while made for some Villain deck control were annoying.

I had never used Cyber-Mod before this. Honestly I've only once used Unleash from Undercover, so the whole idea of sending non-grey Heroes Undercover was very foreign to me. The first game showed it lol ended at a stall there. The second game, Ghost Rider 2099 + Doc Ock really blew up. Got 4 Rares in Alpha's deck - didn't let Thor have a single one lol

Real nailbiters down to the last turn. P1 was all tech cards, P2 all ranged. Tech was way more helpful with Dr. P removing most basic cards and Ultron's Encaphalo-Ray card wreaking havoc. Generic Experimentation drew an absurd amount of bystanders and the special one's effects actually came in clutch a few times. Luckily I was able to hit Iron Man twice times on the last turn due to drawing the bystander that grants Hyperspeed. A Doom 99-focused deck in game one was great, but it just didn't take off well in game two. I tried to use Falcon in the first game to help with drawing sidekicks to get higher attack based on what was in the HQ, but Vision was just more effective.
Had a ton of fun. I love playing the Villains side of things. 2099 heroes and sending things undercover for help in future turns is always a blast.
Phew. Managed to pull off a win on the last possible turn. My wife was not happy that Thor took out Doom's rare that she had been eyeballing for several turns.

Diabolical setup. I've even tried a third attempt with Beast and Goliath that wouldn't count, but even then I could not win the game. I get that thematically Asgardian Warriors make sense for the Scheme, but hell, what a useless Henchmen Fight effect compared to KO one of your heroes. Later in the game it could actually be somewhat useful, but considering that you'd probably invest 3 or even 6 attack to draw 1 card, that card doesn't have the option to do nothing useful. Besides that, I only got rid of all my starters because of getting 4 Soul Sacrifices into deck 1. Dr. Punisher's 4-cost almost always missed his target and didn't show up in HQ much either. I stand by my point that getting KO from Henchmen is super imortant to get powerful decks built. And also, Authoritarian Iron Man isn't a weak Mastermind, so getting rid of starters is arguably even more important.

In my submitted attempt (attempt 2), I came short 1 recruit/attack point to deal the Final Blow on top of two Tactics in turn 39 (which I already didn't think was going to happen anymore). I had a triggered Irresistable Bribe with 9 recruit left over and 2 attack, but that's one short. I had a lot of attack and draw from Dr. Octopus's cards, Captain Britain's cards and Dr. Punisher, the Soldier Supreme's rare. I also got to reveal an Asgardian Warrior from a Tactic, that if it would have been a Master Strike or Scheme Twist would have let me win instead as it would have required 6 fewer attack to get in the final Blow. Those were already all played however, so no luck there. Also both Lokis triggered an additional play of a Master Strike with their FIght or Fail effects, so no extra turns from them either. Perhaps if I would have had  a turn 41 things would have ended differently. Turn 40 was with the other hand/deck and they definitely couldn't make the 18 or 20 attack required for the Final Blow (I don't recall if it was an Asgardian Warrior or a Ms. Marvel as the last card drawn).

Attempt 1 went so much worse that it was actually not even worth noting the exact stats down. I lost by 6th Twist drawn in turn 18 with neither deck/hand able to hit Thor even once.

The Doctor Doom undercover quest went pretty well towards the end (for attempt 2), but Hand 2 could not get rid of starters so even with a few 5 attack 3-costs, not much could be done, at least not towards the Mastermind.

Also, there weren't many obvious choices for Heroes to choose and play. Perhaps I just didn't see the combos that were available. Curious if I will get another last place this week and determined to do better in the final 2 weeks!
Iron Spider is my new nemesis.
N/A

Loved the 5 Heroes and how they meshed....Pagliacci and crew never stood a chance. Magik, Wasp and Ms. Marvel all worked so well together with their classes while Captain Marvel kept the other hand working seamlessly. Lady Thor played both sides as needed. Cards from all heroes worked in either deck and I was quickly able to get some good attack runs. Had a MM hit on turn 9 and thought I could finish in less than 20 turns. For as well as it went...I thought I could improve on my second try. Cards didn't hit quite as effectively but still landed the knockout blow by turn 25. This was the smoothest game of Legendary I've played in quite some time. Great synergy.

Love whenever you can combine Lady Thor & Captain Marvel for some strong synergy. Got luckier in the first game to get some recruitable rares early; never even saw a rare in the second game so it took a lot longer.

This setup didn't prove as challenging as I expected it to.  Pagliacci did up the ante on the Scheme in a kind of interesting way, but didn't add enough difficulty to keep me on the edge of my seat, especially with a reasonably synergized set of default Heroes.  This also might be the first time this tournament that I didn't feel beset by bad luck!  I might have finished a couple turns earlier if I had played less conservatively and just hit the Mastermind rather than keeping the Streets clear, but on the whole I'm happy with how this played out.

This was such a refreshing week. Everything was essentially new to me (I think I played one hero and the MM a si game time previously). Wonder-man and Captain Marvel had amazing symmetry with eachother and easily fit with Lady Thor. My left hand focused on covert (which didn’t do much in the first game), while my right loaded up on strength and range to turn into a total powerhouse. Definitely had a couple extremely close calls in both games, but managed no escapes by pulling twists or bystanders at critical times with a full city. More of this please!
Super fun synergy with Ms. Marvel and Wasp! Interesting tension between Cheering Crowd and wanting to save victory points. Fun time!

Very tough start- Wrecker into Bulldozer, Bulldozer, into 1 Stike/2 Twists. Only 5 Henchmen showed up. Wound stack got down to 2, but we survived.
Took a lot of turns to get going. Heroes were fun to play with but we were drawing scheme twist after scheme twist

This was a fun setup that rewarded the Undercover strategy, and Player 1 was able secure both Deathlok and Coulson's rares which made for strong Witness mitigation and attack power.  It was a strange game given that the first 7 turns saw 5 twists, which is usually a recipe for disaster but in this case worked out fine.
I hate playing Goblin because of his annoying hidden witnesses. Honestly the noir box is one of my least favorite expansions.

I was glad the scheme didn't have a built in timer since this game went down to the wire, I only had three villain deck cards left. There were several very frustrating turns where I had a ton of attack but couldn't hit anything due to hidden witnesses who needed to be rescued with recruit points first. Sif's artifacts were very handy for banking future assistance for turns where I couldn't do much. The Vishanti were great for healing and preventing wounds so I didn't have to feed bystanders to the Vulture.

I finally feel like I am starting to get this game!! Love it!!!
Ancient one was necessary to create some recruit power, maybe Thor wasn't the best choice but he's fun :)
I might have been over thinking it and got unlucky HQs both times but there doesn't seem to be enough recruit to go around for everything that needs it

All hate goes to the scheme lol

Awful first game and lost on turn 39. Right hand only had 3 cards which could attack for 6. Much better 2nd attempt although some early twists came out meaning I had 4 pieces of incriminating evidence. Overall really fun and first time using Asgard set as only recently purchased.

Really didn't like this one. I found the balance was off. The villain deck was too minimal and the hero deck was so full it really made it difficult at times to get any decent cards to hit the mastermind. Also the scheme and mastermind mix was really really hard. I barely won my second game and it all came down to luck.

Both games were a bit frustrating as we took too long to get enough attack to fight against an empowered Ultron, even with a very lean deck thanks to so much KO's going on. Ultron Infinity is basically unbeatable once he gets all the infinity stones.  I understand this thematically but in practice the game is suddenly over due to a second timer on the mastermind.  At the mercy of drawing strikes in late game.  This feels swingy and not much fun when it happens.
Scheme was very interesting.  Was fun balancing Sunlight/Moonlight while at the same time maintaining a limit on the various cost of heroes in the HQ.  Werewolf by Night, Snarling Fangs, allowed manipulation of the HQ which was very useful.
Blood Frenzy is a strong ability!  Morbius, Insatiable Craving, does a lot of damage late game when the victory pile is juicy.  Elsa Bloodstone, Silver Bullets was solid producing 4 attack every time once the city was under control.
I liked this hero team, will play with some of these a bit more after the tournament ;)

Sandals reporting for Iburnicus:
A really good set up. We had heroes that really leaned into Lightshow and we both managed to get it working in both games.
Game 1 was a slog with loads of villains in the City. A Bonded henchman escaped before we could do anything about it, but once we got going we kept that from being a threat. Deathbird was it of reach for a while, with us picking off a tactic here and there. Eventually my wife got Jubilee's Rare due to Aurora and Northstar's Rare and those two cards sped things up.
Game 2 was much faster. I got Dazzler's Uncommon on my turn 1, I Soaring Flight'd her recruit common on my turn 2, then had at least 8 cards per turn for the longest time! I eventually had all three copies of the uncommon, as well as Jubilee's Rare - which was the point I stopped getting extra cards!!!
Overall a lot of fun was had. Glad I could step in to pinch-hit for someone this week!

column36: Deathbird is a really fun mastermind. I lucked out with not many master strikes or twists, and a lot of early henchmen let me build up my decks nicely. Things probably could have been faster, but I wasn't getting the cards I wanted in the HQ. I did get Karnak's rare early on, which was super useful. That hand focused on Jubilee, Polaris, and Karnak, which was a nice combo of drawing a lot of cards so Karnak's rare could let his other cards use their When Recruited abilities.
it was a blast. I picked up Jublie's, Karnak's, and Banshee's rare across both hands. last few turns were pretty awesome.
1st attempt we got absolutely annihilated, you could say everything went to... "Shi't" ;). The 2nd attempt we leaned into the Berserk strategy instead of clone and were able to pull out a win.

My first play was concetrating on getting anall lightshow deck woth Dazzler piercing to take of Deathbird. It worked, but took too long with 6 escapes. I swapped out Aurora/Northstar for Medusa, and this worked better, with the Inhuman hand able to retain the Throner's Favour, and Karnak having 10 Imperial Guard in his victory pile for huge boosts to attack totles.

column36:
Sooo muuch Shi'ar... having both Shi'ar Villain groups AND both Shi'ar Henchmen groups just ensured that Deathbird was always going to be stronger than expected.
This was honestly quite a fun setup, although it did end up being a big of a slog just because so many things caused more stuff to enter the City and/or make things in the City harder to Fight. It was a little frustrating that all Deathbird's Tactics just jumped back into the City, and you also lose those points unless you kill them again.
Anyway, I chose Stepford Cuckoos because they seemed fun and like they'd help ramp up the damage fairly efficiently. I also picked Venompool for extra damage and because I figured there'd be a lot of Bystander action. Both of those choices paid off reasonably well. With the Cuckoos and Shatterstar cloning all over the place, and the X-men Bystanders getting gained as Heroes when rescued, both players ended up with very thick decks. There wasn't a huge amount of hero KO available to get rid of starter cards, but it turns out just drowning them in a bunch of upgraded cards is almost as good, at least if you don't care too much about strong synergies.
Looooong game!  Piercing energy was the saving grace.

game one was brutal, lost on turn like 10. game two went better if long, one player 2 went full light show on Jubilee, while player one went sorta everything else, and managed to do all right. kept hoping Karnak would show up, as if i counted right there were technically 6 villain groups in this setup, saw 4 of his cards the whole game and none of them was the attack card :(
Fast game and stress free game. Was great to combine Deadpool and Wolverine as they worked well together, as it they should (shouldn't?).
I was jealous of other setups in the other pods. Love is Blind in my pods.
The game was pretty straight forward. Played 2 rounds and they came out almost identical.  Hard a hard time getting things going early on with the wounds accumulating.

column36: Good fun.  Wish I wasn't rushed to do it and had time for attempt two.

Not only core is not fun anymore to play and I understand that it is difficult to set up games where everyone could have similar sets but it is not enjoyable to be sent to the bottom of the groups when you are doing good. Really difficult to enjoy the tournament when that happens.

I really struggled to refine my decks in both games. The Villain deck/Mastermind was very unforgiving with lots of wounds with barely any opportunities to KO cards. I went Human Torch and Hulk in game 1 to focus on accumulating and KO'ing wounds, but I found that my deck just got clogged with SHIELD cards (when I hit the Loki tactic to KO 4 cards, my discard pile was empty). I swapped out Hulk for Black Widow in game 2, and that went a lot better. Unfortunately, some bad timing with Scheme Twists meant that I wasn't able to recruit many of her best cards to set up my decks well. My final score for both games was very very close. I was a bit faster in game 2, but defeated some higher value villains in game 1. I decided to report game 1 just because I had two villains escape in game 2. 
If I could try this a third time, I might try Invisible Woman and Black Widow to really double-down on the KO ability. Though that build may have struggled with attack value? I might try it just for the heck of it.
This game was a classic example of "I have great cards in my decks, if only I could draw them more often." One turn Player 2 had 17 attack, then the next round they drew 4 shield agents and 2 troopers...

column36: Heralds of Galactus are a nightmare when they come out early which must be why they have more VPs than Loki as MM. Fortunately MS stayed lownin the deck so wounds weren't really a problem. Black Widow and Deadpool played nice on one hand and Thing / Wolverine were good too. My tweener Mr Fantastic helped a bit with Things activation but not much else. I keep low scoring though... More to learn.
I actually feel embarrassed to even submit these results for our game lol. However, despite our embarrassing loss, the format was just incredibly boring for both of us.
